majorFindings: The uploaded ZIP contains a locked flavor/add-on system and staff rules, but it does not contain complete base food item pricing for the TMA menu.
Base menu item placeholders were created with PRICE_REQUIRED_FROM_MENU.
The pasted Heat Economy System was applied as heat-system.json and linked into customer-tags/order-schema.
Bottle prices in app-ready addons.json use the uploaded JSON package: Mango Mayhem $9.50 and Strawberry Armageddon $10.95.
The pasted heat-system text includes different bottle prices in different sections ($12.50/$9). Those were not used because uploaded JSON package prices were prioritized for product prices.
Uploaded index.js contains a heat/spice implementation, but it currently appears unsafe to treat as final production logic without patching.
indexJsIssuesObserved: Heat points are updated during order placement, while locked rule says only PAID + COMPLETED + not refunded orders should earn points.
Spice eligibility reads stats.completedOrders, but initial profile uses stats.ordersCompleted. This can block/alter reward eligibility.
Hot sauce callback branch references variables such as chatId, msgId, draft, and userId in a scope where they are not defined in the uploaded code path.
HEAT_PRICES_USD in index.js uses revenge: 1.25 and nuclear: 2.50, while the pasted locked heat system says Revenge +$1.00 and Nuclear +$1.50.

Reality lock comment says dry rub marker is cactus, but current saved brand memory says the custom mortar-and-pestle icon is the locked dry-rub glyph. This should be manually reconciled before UI finalization.
The uploaded index.js uses node-telegram-bot-api/polling bot flow, not a Telegram Mini App frontend architecture yet.

usedPricesFromZip: Dry rub standard upgrade $0.50 per serving
D5/D6 premium hot sauce drizzle $1.00 per serving

nextRequiredAction: Review generated JSON files, then decide whether Phase 4 should build a TMA frontend or first patch the existing polling bot.
